title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: PLA aircraft sorties spiked to 24 on April 21 — the highest non-exercise daily count since Justice Mission-2025 in December 2025 — with 11 crossing the median line into Taiwan's northern, southwestern, and eastern ADIZ simultaneously, following the Liaoning (CV-16) carrier transit on April 20 (12 PLAN vessels). The two-day PLA escalation operation unfolds 23 days before the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing (May 14-15), where Xi has reportedly called for 'prudence on US arms sales to Taiwan'; analysts assess Beijing is seeking incremental gains to weaken Taiwan-US ties. Three gap-day event files were created for April 14, 15, and 21; map-lines and KPIs updated to reflect the escalation pattern; claims and political sections updated with summit context.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 20, 2026
summary: PLA aircraft carrier Liaoning (CV-16) transited the Taiwan Strait on April 20 with 5 aircraft sorties and 12 PLAN vessels — the highest ship count since the April 11 post-KMT-summit surge — as 4 sorties crossed the median line into Taiwan's SW ADIZ. JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi made the first Japanese destroyer strait transit under PM Takaichi on April 17, prompting China to label it a 'deliberate provocation' and launch East China Sea 'joint combat readiness patrols' on April 18. Taiwan's legislature broke a 230-day defense budget deadlock on April 16, advancing the NT$1.25T special budget; President Lai inspected Kaohsiung army training command on April 17. Four new event files created covering April 16, 17, 18, and 20; map-lines, map-points, and KPIs updated.

title: Breaking: SCMP analysis: PLA is developing stealth drone swarms for boat attacks on Taiwan
summary: SCMP analysis: PLA is developing stealth drone swarms for boat attacks on Taiwan, detailing new asymmetric naval threat doctrine from the Northern Theater Command.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: China's Eastern Theater Command announced 'joint combat readiness patrols' in the East China Sea following the transit of Japan's JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi through the Taiwan Strait the previous day.

title: Breaking: China labels the passage of a Japanese Maritime Self-Defense Force warship throu
summary: China labels the passage of a Japanese Maritime Self-Defense Force warship through the Taiwan Strait a 'provocation,' escalating diplomatic tensions with Japan.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Legislative Yuan reached a landmark agreement to begin deliberating the government's NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.6 billion) special defense budget after a 230-day deadlock.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 5 PLA aircraft sorties and 6 PLAN vessels plus 3 official ships operating around Taiwan as of April 15, 2026.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 2 PLA aircraft sorties and 9 PLAN vessels plus 1 official ship operating around Taiwan as of April 14, 2026.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 12, 2026
summary: PLA air activity dropped sharply to 2 sorties on April 12 — the final day of KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's six-day China visit — down from 17 sorties on April 11, consistent with PLA's pressure-and-dialogue dual-track strategy of peaking coercion after high-profile diplomatic events and then rapidly reducing as visits conclude. Cheng departed Beijing Capital International Airport for Taiwan, seen off by CPC Taiwan Work Office Head Song Tao. China's Taiwan Affairs Office simultaneously announced a 10-measure cross-strait package including resumed individual travel (Shanghai/Fujian to Taiwan), normalized direct flights, agricultural/fishery trade, and a regular CPC-KMT communication mechanism — all conditioned on the 1992 Consensus; DPP government rejected the package; KMT welcomed it. 8 PLAN vessels and 4 official ships continued operating around Taiwan. 32 days remain to the Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14-15).

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 11, 2026
summary: PLA surged to 17 aircraft sorties on April 11 — the highest since the March 29 peak of 19 — with 15 of 17 crossing the median line into Taiwan's northern and southwestern ADIZ, alongside 7 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship. The dramatic post-Xi-KMT summit surge (from 7 sorties on April 10) confirms PLA's dual-track pressure-and-dialogue strategy: the historic Xi Jinping–Cheng Li-wun meeting on April 10 did not temper military coercion against Taiwan's elected DPP government. On April 11 (Day 5 of the KMT visit), Cheng held a press conference at the China World Hotel in Beijing defending the Xi meeting as 'deterrence through dialogue' and pledging cross-strait engagement resumption if KMT returns to power in 2028. KMT lawmakers continue boycotting Taiwan's special defense budget cross-caucus negotiations; Foreign Policy noted 'Taiwan's Budget Crisis Gives China an Opening.' 33 days remain to the Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14-15).

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 10, 2026
summary: Xi Jinping met KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on April 10 — the first formal CPC-KMT leader summit in approximately a decade — with Xi declaring 'Taiwan independence is the chief culprit' undermining cross-strait peace and vowing it will 'never be tolerated.' Cheng called for 'reconciliation' and reaffirmed the 1992 Consensus; Taiwan's DPP President Lai Ching-te sharply condemned the meeting, saying 'compromising with authoritarian regimes only sacrifices sovereignty,' while the MAC urged Beijing to engage Taiwan's elected government directly. PLA surged to 6 aircraft sorties on April 9 ahead of the Beijing summit — 5 of 6 crossing the median line into northern and southwestern ADIZ sectors, up sharply from 2 on April 8 — with 8 PLAN vessels operating in multi-vector posture. The Xi-KMT summit unfolds 34 days before the scheduled Trump-Xi summit in Beijing on May 14-15.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Thu Apr 9, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported (as of 0600 local time April 9, 2026) detecting 6 PLA aircraft sorties alongside 8 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship operating around Taiwan. KMT Delegation Arrives in Beijing — Day 3; Xi Jinping Meeting Confirmed for April 10.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 08, 2026
summary: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun paid homage at the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum in Nanjing on April 8 — Day 2 of her April 7-12 mainland China visit — calling for 'seeds of peace' across the strait while Taiwan's Mainland Affairs Council criticized her 'veiled' reference to the Republic of China as insufficient given Beijing's information controls. PLA upticked to 2 aircraft sorties (from 1 on April 7), with both sorties crossing the Taiwan Strait median line and entering the northern and southeastern ADIZ sectors — the first northern sector incursion since the late-March spike — alongside 9 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship in multi-vector posture. A potential Xi Jinping meeting in Beijing remains officially unconfirmed. The visit continues with 36 days remaining until the Trump-Xi Beijing summit on May 14-15.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 07, 2026
summary: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun arrived in Shanghai at noon on April 7, beginning the first mainland China visit by a sitting KMT party chair in approximately a decade — calling it a 'journey for peace.' The April 7–12 trip (Shanghai → Nanjing → Beijing) includes the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum on April 8 and an expected Xi Jinping meeting in Beijing, unfolding 37 days before the planned Trump-Xi summit on May 14–15. PLA kept air activity at 1 sortie on April 7 while maintaining 8 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship in a multi-vector naval posture — a combined pressure-and-dialogue signal consistent with not overshadowing the diplomatic channel on the KMT chair's arrival day. April running totals stood at 205 PLA aircraft tracks and 294 ship tracks. Events, map-lines, map-points, KPIs, political, and meta sections were updated.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun departs for China tomorrow (April 7), beginning the first KMT chair visit to mainland China in a decade — a trip that is sparking internal KMT unease as of April 6, with Taiwan News reporting party divisions over risks of Beijing exploitation ahead of the May 14-15 Trump-Xi summit. PLA maintained 3 sorties around Taiwan on April 5 (all crossing the median line), a minor uptick from 2 on April 3, while year-to-date 2026 PLA ADIZ sorties are 46.5% below the same 2025 period. DPP legislators and US senators urged the KMT caucus on April 4 to pass Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget before Cheng's China departure. Poland issued its first-ever public statement on Taiwan Strait tensions on April 4, opposing any unilateral status quo change — reflecting expanding European engagement. Events for April 4-6 created; map-lines, KPIs, political, and meta sections updated.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: PLA 3 Sorties Around Taiwan April 5 — All Cross Median Line; YTD Incursions Down 46.5%. Analysis: KMT Chair Cheng's China Trip 'Fraught with Risk' — Internal Party Unease Deepens Two Days Before Departure.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: DPP Legislators Urgently Press KMT to Pass Defense Budget Before Cheng's April 7 China Departure. US Senators Publicly Back Lai's Full NT$1.25T Special Defense Budget in TaiwanPlus Statements. Poland's government issued its first-ever public statement expressing concern about Taiwan Strait tensions on April 4, 2026, according to Vision Times.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 03, 2026
summary: PLA air activity de-escalated sharply to 2 sorties on April 3 — down from 11 on April 1 — continuing an oscillating spike-and-retract pattern since the March 29 peak of 19 sorties, with 7 PLAN vessels and 2 official ships still operating. A PLA Navy warship warned a US Navy P-8 Poseidon near Taiwan's Hengchun Peninsula at 0647 local time, claiming the aircraft had entered China's 24nm contiguous zone — the latest PLA tactic to pressure US ISR operations near Taiwan. KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's April 7-12 China visit itinerary was confirmed: Shanghai → Nanjing (Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, April 8) → Beijing, with a Xi Jinping meeting confirmed — allowing Beijing to leverage Sun Yat-sen symbolism to frame cross-strait relations within a Chinese historical unity narrative ahead of the May 14-15 Trump-Xi summit.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: PLA spiked to 11 sorties on April 1 — all entering Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ with 7 PLAN vessels — reversing the 1-sortie baseline of March 30-31 and signaling resumed pressure coinciding with KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's confirmed April 7-12 China visit. The Cheng visit, the first KMT chair trip to China in 10 years, follows a formal Xi Jinping invitation and is assessed by Taiwan national security sources as a strategic attempt to create an 'illusion of unification momentum' for President Trump ahead of the May 14-15 Trump-Xi Beijing summit. The defense budget cross-party gap remains unresolved with 42 days to the summit, raising the stakes for whether Taiwan secures its procurement timelines. Events, map lines, KPIs, political, claims, and meta sections updated.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: PLA Spikes to 11 Sorties on April 1 — Breaking March 30–31 One-Sortie Baseline. KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun Confirms April 7–12 China Visit — First KMT Chair Trip in 10 Years. Taiwan Security Source: KMT China Visit Designed to Create 'Illusion of Unification Momentum' for Trump.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Mar 31, 2026
summary: PLA activity dropped sharply to 1 sortie on both March 30 and March 31 — from the 19-sortie peak on March 29 — confirming the late-March escalation as a bounded spike-and-retract signal rather than the start of a sustained campaign. A bipartisan US Senate Foreign Relations Committee delegation (Sens. Shaheen, Curtis, Rosen, Tillis) arrived in Taipei on March 30 and publicly pressed Taiwan's legislature to pass the stalled NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget. KMT Taichung Mayor Lu Shiow-yen broke with the official KMT caucus position, signaling an NT$800B–NT$1T compromise range — the first crack in KMT unity on the defense budget. Political section updated with AIT Director Raymond Greene and KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun; claims section updated to reflect the narrowing inter-party gap under sustained US pressure.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Update — Mar 30, 2026
summary: PLA reached a 19-sortie single-day peak on March 29 — the highest count of the late-March escalation spike — with 13 sorties crossing the median line across all four ADIZ sectors simultaneously and 11 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan. This completed a three-day surge (6 → 15 → 19 sorties) that represents the sharpest week-over-week acceleration since March 25. AIT Director Raymond Greene publicly backed Taiwan's NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget on March 29 and called for 5% GDP defense spending by 2030, the most direct US endorsement of the full bill yet. Taiwan's army also conducted combat readiness drills on the Matsu islands and announced expanded missile output for the T-Dome air defense system. KPI, map line, and claims sections were updated to reflect the latest sortie peak and the AIT Director's public pressure on Taiwan's legislature.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Sun Mar 29, 2026
summary: Taiwan MND Detects 19 PLA Aircraft Sorties — Highest Single-Day Count of Late-March Spike. AIT Director Greene Publicly Backs 5% GDP Defense Target and NT$1.25T Special Budget. The Taiwan Army conducted combat readiness drills on the Matsu islands on March 29, in the context of elevated PLA activity in the adjacent sea lanes.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Update — Mar 28, 2026
summary: PLA conducted a significant 15-sortie ADIZ crossing on March 28, with 11 sorties penetrating all four sectors simultaneously (northern, central, southwestern, and eastern) for the first time since March 18 — a spike from March 27's moderate 6 sorties. Aircraft confirmed: J-10 and J-16 fighters plus a KJ-500 AEW&C; 7 PLAN vessels and 2 official ships also operated around Taiwan. In parallel, two newly commissioned Type 055 guided-missile destroyers (Dongguan and Anqing) were assigned to PLA's Eastern Theater Command and conducted their inaugural live-fire drill in the East China Sea. PRC announced a 7% defense budget increase to ~$278 billion for 2026. Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget remains stalled in the legislature; the $14B PAC-3/NASAMS arms package continues to await the Trump-Xi summit rescheduled to May 14–15.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: PLA maintained a moderate 6-sorties/day pattern on March 27 (4 crossing the median line), consistent with the post-NPC cadence, with a notable spike to 16 sorties on March 25 (13 crossing). Three gap-day event files were created covering March 20–21 and March 27, capturing the full week's PLA aerial and naval activity. A US envoy publicly pressed Taiwan's legislature to pass the stalled NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget, of which only 2 articles cleared committee by March 27; the opposition KMT and TPP back far smaller NT$380–400B alternatives. Taiwan's HIMARS letters of offer and acceptance were signed before the March 26 deadline, securing delivery queue placement. The Trump-Xi summit has been rescheduled to May 14–15, continuing to delay the $14B PAC-3/NASAMS arms package. TSMC Q4 2025 revenue hit $33.7B with Q1 2026 guidance up 38% YoY, and a new US-Taiwan trade deal cut tariffs to 15% in exchange for a $500B semiconductor manufacturing commitment.

title: Taiwan Conflict Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: PLA aerial activity around Taiwan spiked to 16 sorties on March 25 (13 crossing the median line) following a quieter period of just 3 sorties on March 24, suggesting continued volatility in Chinese military signaling. Taiwan's HIMARS letter of offer and acceptance deadline expires today, with parliament having unanimously authorized signing on March 13 — covering $9 billion in US arms packages including HIMARS, TOWS, M109A7 howitzers, and Javelins. A $14 billion record arms package comprising PAC-3 and NASAMS interceptors remains ready for President Trump's approval but has been held back pending the now-postponed Trump-Xi Beijing summit, which Trump delayed 5–6 weeks due to the Iran conflict. Defense Minister Wellington Koo confirmed F-16V deliveries are expected to begin in September 2026, with the US establishing a special project team to accelerate the $8.28 billion backlog of delayed weapons shipments.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 16 sorties of Chinese military aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels, and 2 official ships operating around Taiwan on March 25. Taiwan Defense Minister: US Has 'High Urgency' in Speeding Arms Deliveries.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 3 sorties of PLA aircraft, 9 PLAN vessels, and 1 official ship operating around Taiwan on March 24.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 7 Chinese naval vessels and 3 official ships operating around Taiwan's territorial waters on March 23.

title: Taiwan Strait Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: PLA military activity around Taiwan has fully resumed after a two-week lull during China's National People's Congress. On March 21, 26 PLA aircraft were detected operating around Taiwan with 16 crossing the median line, accompanied by 7 warships. Taiwan's domestic political gridlock continues, with the opposition-controlled legislature blocking defense spending increases. Washington's mixed signals on Taiwan policy are being closely monitored by Beijing. In December 2025, China conducted its most extensive military drills to date, simulating a complete island blockade.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Sat Mar 21, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 8 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan on March 21.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 6 sorties of PLA aircraft and 8 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan on March 20.

title: Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Fri Mar 13, 2026
summary: Taiwan Parliament Unanimously Authorizes $9B US Arms Deal Signing.
